sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Acne on the face.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports a two-year history of facial acne, which began approximately two years ago and is present most days. She has been using over-the-counter washes and Persa-gel, which provides some improvement. She reports that her acne flares with increased sweating, particularly since starting school sports in the fall. She consistently uses sunscreen with SPF 30 or higher when outdoors. She uses a cleanser and toner in the morning, followed by Persa-gel. She applies adapalene cream in the evening to affected areas. She reports experiencing some sensitivity to the adapalene and dryness. She also reports multiple benign nevi on her trunk and extremities.
Findings
A full exam was performed today, except for under the underwear and under the bra. The remainder of the examination is normal. Mild to moderate acne is present, predominantly comedonal with small inflammatory components. Multiple benign nevi were noted on the trunk and extremities. Scattered skin-colored papules, open and closed comedones, and erythremaous papules are present on the face, primarily on the forehead and central cheeks and chin.
Assessment
The patient presents with moderate acne.
Plan
I would like to start with a topical therapy. Order a topical therapy for acne treatment.
